title: Sentinel Shield control waivers
description: Explicit, owner-bound, expiring waivers for a REQUIRED tool/control. Distinct from accepted-risks (which suppress finding gates): a control-waiver downgrades a required-tool failure (unavailable / not-configured / disabled) to a prominently-reported, time-boxed waiver. Consumed by scripts/enforce-gates.sh. NEVER auto-applied: an unexpired waiver only changes the report wording, the underlying control is still flagged. A waiver does NOT suppress findings; use accepted-risks for that.

version.description: Schema version. Only "1" is supported; consumers fail closed on anything else.

waiver.description: A single control-waiver record. All fields are mandatory: a waiver must name the tool, justify itself, identify an owner and an approver, be dated, expire, and link a tracking issue. additionalProperties:false so typos/extra fields are rejected.

tool.description: Tool/one-of-group key this waiver covers (e.g. 'larastan', 'php-tests'). Must be a single shell-safe token: letters/digits/._- only, so it can never split into multiple controls.

justification.description: Why this required control is temporarily waived.

owner.description: Accountable owner for resolving the underlying gap.

approved_by.description: Who approved the waiver (must differ from owner in practice; not enforced by schema).

created_at.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) the waiver was created.

expires_at.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD). The waiver only applies while expires_at >= today; expired waivers are ignored and the control fails.

tracking_issue.description: Issue/ticket URL or id tracking remediation of the waived control.
